skill.name: plugin-structure
skill.description: Scaffold and organize Claude Code plugins with correct directories, manifests, component patterns, auto-discovery, and portable path references.
skill.summary: Guides creation of well-structured Claude Code plugins from manifest setup to component organization.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official Anthropic source reviewed with minimal audit scope. Static risk indicators are documentation examples for hooks, MCP servers, paths, and environment variables, not active execution logic.

content.value_statement: Plugin projects can fail when manifests, directories, and component paths are inconsistent. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through correct plugin structure and portable organization.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required Claude Code plugin directory layout.
Organizes commands, agents, skills, hooks, scripts, and MCP server files.
Shows when to use default auto-discovery and custom component paths.
Provides minimal, standard, and advanced plugin examples.
limitations: Does not publish plugins to a marketplace by itself.
Does not validate every runtime dependency for generated hook scripts.
Does not replace security review for third-party plugin code.
Focuses on Claude Code plugin architecture, not general extension systems.

use_cases.title: Create a first plugin
use_cases.description: Scaffold a small plugin with a manifest and one command using the expected Claude Code layout.

use_cases.title: Standardize team plugins
use_cases.description: Define consistent names, folders, metadata, and portable paths across shared internal plugins.

use_cases.title: Design advanced integrations
use_cases.description: Plan plugins that combine hooks, scripts, MCP servers, shared libraries, and multiple skills.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner plugin scaffold
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when starting a small plugin from zero.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a minimal Claude Code plugin structure for a command named review-notes. Include the required folders and explain each file.

prompt_templates.title: Manifest review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a plugin exists but the manifest may be incomplete.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my plugin manifest and directory layout. Point out missing required fields, nonportable paths, and auto-discovery issues.

prompt_templates.title: Component organization plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a plugin needs several commands, agents, skills, and hooks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a standard Claude Code plugin layout for testing automation. Include commands, agents, skills, hooks, scripts, and naming conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced architecture audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before shipping a complex plugin with integrations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this advanced plugin architecture for maintainability, manifest correctness, portable paths, component discovery, and security review gaps.

output_examples.input: I need a plugin with one command and no scripts.
output: A minimal layout with one manifest and one command file.
A short explanation of why extra folders are unnecessary.
Naming guidance for the command and plugin directory.

output_examples.input: My hooks cannot find their helper scripts after install.
output: A diagnosis focused on portable path references.
A recommendation to use the plugin root environment variable.
A checklist for verifying script location and permissions.

output_examples.input: We need a plugin with commands, agents, skills, and MCP servers.
output: A production-ready folder plan for each component type.
Manifest guidance for metadata and custom paths.
Operational notes for dependency documentation and release versioning.

best_practices: Use default component directories unless custom paths clearly improve organization.
Reference internal files with ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for portability.
Keep manifests concise and document dependencies near the components that need them.
anti_patterns: Do not place commands, agents, skills, or hooks inside the manifest directory.
Do not hardcode absolute local paths in hooks, scripts, or MCP settings.
Do not create vague component names such as utils, misc, or temp.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Claude Code plugin structures, manifests, component folders, and portable path references.

faq.question: Does it generate executable plugin logic?
faq.answer: It can guide file placement and examples, but runtime logic still needs normal implementation and testing.

faq.question: Which component types are covered?
faq.answer: It covers commands, agents, skills, hooks, scripts, MCP server configuration, and shared support files.

faq.question: Can it help with existing plugins?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review structure, manifest fields, naming, discovery paths, and portability issues.

faq.question: Does it publish plugins?
faq.answer: No. It prepares plugin structure but does not handle marketplace publishing or release approval.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. It is official documentation guidance and contains no active malicious behavior.
